description: An unprocessable message redelivers forever, blocking the consumer and stalling all later work.
body: Failure mode: a malformed or permanently-failing message is nacked and redelivered indefinitely; with strict ordering or a single in-flight slot it blocks every subsequent message. Detection signals: no attempt counter; no dead-letter queue; one message reappears in logs continuously; queue depth grows while throughput drops to zero. Do not let failures redeliver without bound. Safer fallback: count attempts, route to a DLQ with error context after the cap, alert, and continue; add a test that a poison message reaches the DLQ without stalling the queue.
